summary: Approved after Hermes review and an OpenCode GLM-5.1 read-only implementation gate. The change adds a bounded OpenAI-compatible OCR provider, registry selection, env override support, example config, and behavior-focused tests without adding dependencies, secrets, parser wiring, frontend changes, or remote changes.

findings.description: OCR provider exists and is registry-selectable, but scanned PDF rasterization and OCR invocation from parser/pipeline are intentionally out of scope for this bounded slice.
Go tests could not be run from the Hermes supervisor environment because go is not installed on PATH.

accepted_changes: Added OpenAIOCRProvider with image byte limits, response byte limits, max_tokens, retry/backoff, context cancellation, MIME detection, base64 data URL payloads, language-aware prompts, and plain-text/JSON response parsing.
Changed OCR registry initialization so endpoint-configured OCR providers use the real OpenAI-compatible implementation and fail fast if model is missing; endpoint-less providers remain stub fallback.
Added OCR MODEL env override support and safe underscore provider env var prefixes while preserving legacy hyphenated prefix lookup.
Updated dev example OCR config with OpenAI-compatible provider defaults and no committed secrets.

security_review.notes: No provider keys or GitHub tokens were committed. API keys remain blank/configurable via environment variables. Origin remains https://github.com/unalkalkan/TwelveReader.git.

quality_review.notes: Implementation is bounded to OCR provider/config/registry behavior and follows existing provider helper patterns.

validation.notes: go binary not installed on PATH in Hermes environment
